Military Mar 26, 2026

This aircraft offers some impressive offensive and defensive capabilities.

The A-10's redundant systems and armor distribution enable sustained operations in high-threat environments where conventional aircraft cannot survive.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Authorities Searching For American Airlines Flight Attendant Reported Missing In Colombia

The disappearance of an American Airlines flight attendant in Medellin, Colombia, has left his family desperate for answers.

International crew safety incidents highlight vulnerabilities for airline staff during layovers in high-risk destinations and may prompt carriers to reassess layover protocols.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy...

IXPE's X-ray polarimetry data reveals supernova magnetic field structures that traditional imaging cannot detect, advancing understanding of high-energy astrophysics phenomena.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Colorado’s Rocky Mountain Metro Airport Launches Unleaded Avgas

UL94 sales begin with state-backed price support and STC reimbursement.

State-backed pricing and STC reimbursement programs in Colorado could serve as a model for accelerating unleaded avgas adoption across other regions facing supply constraints.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Only 54% Full: Delta Air Lines' 10 Emptiest Long-Haul Routes Revealed

How many of them can you guess? There are some intriguing surprises.

Delta's underutilized long-haul routes suggest capacity misalignment or weak demand that could signal operational inefficiency or network strategy adjustments ahead.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from post-accident investigation to continuous hazard identification, reducing incidents before they occur rather than learning from disasters.

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic supersonic fighter production capability strengthens its defense-industrial base and reduces dependency on foreign military aircraft suppliers.

Aircraft availability disruptions from manufacturer defects can rapidly reverse airline profitability, highlighting operational risk concentration in modern fleets.

AMA Advocacy: H.R. 6460 passes U.S. House of Representatives

On Tuesday, March 24, H.R. 6460, a bill championed by AMA that will simplify altitude...

H.R. 6460's passage streamlines regulatory barriers for recreational drone operators by adjusting altitude restrictions, potentially expanding the addressable market for consumer UAS manufacturers.

Part 5's safety risk assessment framework requires operators to quantify risk likelihood and severity systematically, moving beyond qualitative judgments to defensible safety decisions.

Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain

Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Friday, March 20, 2026 Curiosity has just concluded a very intense week of science observations and engineering activities, as it wraps up its monthslong investigation...

Curiosity's extended boxwork terrain investigation demonstrates sustained rover productivity over extended mission timelines, informing strategies for long-duration planetary exploration vehicles.

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

The FAA's records demand signals potential enforcement action that could affect state airport funding mechanisms beyond California, setting precedent for federal oversight of state aviation infrastructure investment.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's Artemis II lunar mission targeting April 1 launch represents the critical test flight validating human-rated deep-space architecture before lunar surface operations.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure at RNO requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexity that differs from standard departure operations at the airport.

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's 360-degree FPV capability combined with omnidirectional obstacle sensing enables safer, more immersive aerial content creation that previously required multiple camera angles or manual risk management.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's 3,500 monthly departures from Austin signals a major hub expansion that could reshape competitive dynamics in central Texas air travel markets.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

Redirecting Patriot interceptors from Ukraine to Middle East operations could create critical air defense gaps for allied nations, complicating military logistics and strategic commitments.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as essential training hubs that funnel pilots into the commercial aviation pipeline while maintaining consistent air carrier traffic.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Consistent cabin crew excellence has become Virgin Australia's competitive differentiator, suggesting service quality directly influences customer loyalty and brand valuation in competitive markets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

Atlanta's sustained 100M+ passenger volume reflects cargo and connecting traffic resilience beyond pure leisure demand, signaling infrastructure strain across US hubs.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Comet spin-reversal findings demonstrate how outgassing forces reshape small celestial bodies, insights applicable to spacecraft trajectory prediction near comets.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight paths offers immediate efficiency gains that can accommodate projected traffic growth without proportional emissions increases.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian forces have adapted decades-old training aircraft into armed platforms, demonstrating that low-tech solutions can effectively counter modern drone threats when conventional air defenses are unavailable.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

$147,000 Damage: Military KC-46A Tanker Rips Up 25 Foot Section Of Airport Tarmac

The continuous high temperature and pressure from the engine blast affected the integrity of the parking ramp, resulting in this damage.

KC-46A engine exhaust temperatures are degrading airport infrastructure during routine operations, signaling potential maintenance base design vulnerabilities across the tanker fleet.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The Boeing 777-300ER’s Windows Are Compared To The Airbus A350

Did you know that the older Triple-Seven has larger windows than a 21st-century clean-sheet carbon-composite widebody?

Boeing's older 777-300ER windows exceed the A350's despite being a 1990s design, suggesting window size prioritizes cabin experience over modern aerodynamic optimization.

See Simple Flying →

AirAsia Philippines risks losing licenses over $14.5 million unpaid fees

The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has issued a final demand letter to AirAsia Philippines for…...

Regulatory non-compliance and financial disputes can escalate to operational shutdown threats, underscoring the critical importance of maintaining regulatory relationships alongside financial obligations.

Volotea closes smaller than anticipated funding round following strong results

Regional low-cost carrier Volotea has completed its latest capital increase round. The airline announced in September 2024 that…...

Volotea's decision to close a smaller funding round despite operational strength suggests confidence in profitability rather than expansion capital needs.

The Airline That Introduced Lie-Flat Business Class Seats To The World

Lie-flat business class seats became a competitive necessity that transformed premium cabin economics, forcing airlines to invest billions in product differentiation to justify higher fares.

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Bombardier delivers first Global 8000 to NetJets

Bombardier has handed over the first Global 8000 business jet to NetJets, marking the fleet launch customer’s entry…...

NetJets' selection as launch customer signals strong market confidence in the Global 8000's competitive positioning against rival ultra-long-range business jets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 17-Hour Nonstop: Emirates' 10 Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Emirates is the world's second-largest long-haul operator, after United Airlines. In 2026, the carrier has an average of 134 daily long-haul passenger departures from its Dubai hub. Over half of the carrier's total flights fall into this category.

Emirates' ultra-long-haul network demonstrates how hub consolidation and aircraft efficiency enable carriers to dominate point-to-point markets that competitors cannot sustain profitably.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

United Airlines transforms devil’s chariot into premium CRJ-450

LOS ANGELES — Before the COVID-19 pandemic upended the airline industry, one-third of all United Airlines’ domestic US departures were on 50-seat regional jets. While that number has fallen to just 10% today, United isn’t resting on its laurels as demand for premium travel products remains high....

United's premium CRJ-450 retrofit addresses the paradox of declining regional jet utilization while capitalizing on elevated demand for higher-margin cabin products in post-pandemic travel markets.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Mar 26, 2026

South Korea rolls out first mass-production KF-21 Boramae

South Korea has rolled out the first mass-produced KF-21 Boramae fighter jet, completing a major production milestone for…...

South Korea's transition from prototype to mass production of the KF-21 signals regional military aviation capability expansion and reduces dependence on foreign fighter imports.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's staggered global launch strategy for Avata 360 reflects ongoing regulatory friction in the US market, where geopolitical constraints continue to fragment the consumer drone industry's product availability timeline.

See DroneDJ →

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Etihad Airways' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Two of the routes are brand-new this year, while another started in 2025. Learn more here!

Etihad's expansion into ultra-long routes reflects growing airline confidence in fuel efficiency and passenger demand for point-to-point connections over hub transfers.
